nodes.title: Tokenize bracket keys into a clean path
nodes.why: Bracket syntax like user[name] and filters[tag][] needs a consistent path shape before assignment.
nodes.alternative: Use manual string splitting by [ and ] for each key.
nodes.tradeoff: Regex is compact and reliable, but slightly harder to read at first glance.

nodes.title: Treat empty segment as array push
nodes.why: An empty segment ([]) means append to array, not assign to a named object key.
nodes.alternative: Reject [] syntax and only support repeated plain keys.
nodes.tradeoff: More logic in assignment, but it supports common querystring patterns.

nodes.title: Preserve repeated keys by promoting to arrays
nodes.why: When a key appears again, we keep both values instead of losing earlier data.
nodes.alternative: Last value wins and earlier values are overwritten.
nodes.tradeoff: Output shape can become string-or-array, but no user input is dropped.

nodes.title: Create object or array based on next token
nodes.why: Looking ahead one segment prevents building the wrong container type at nested levels.
nodes.alternative: Always create objects and fix shape later.
nodes.tradeoff: Small look-ahead complexity, but cleaner and fewer post-fixes.

nodes.title: Split each pair on the first = only
nodes.why: Values may contain =, so splitting once keeps the full value intact.
nodes.alternative: Use part.split('=') and join the rest back.
nodes.tradeoff: Slicing is a bit manual, but behavior is predictable.

nodes.title: Normalize + and decode percent-encoding
nodes.why: Many querystrings use + for spaces, so normalization keeps user text readable.
nodes.alternative: Decode percent-encoding only and keep + as a literal plus sign.
nodes.tradeoff: Opinionated parsing choice, but aligns with common form behavior.

nodes.title: Keep write logic in one assign function
nodes.why: All structure decisions happen in one place, so behavior stays consistent for every pair.
nodes.alternative: Inline assignment logic inside the parse loop.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra function call, but much easier to maintain and test.
